title: Publications
text: My research covers underground hydrogen storage, salt cavern engineering, renewable energy integration, and geotechnical safety. My name is shown in bold; an asterisk indicates the corresponding author as reported in my CV.

title: Weizheng Bai
text: I am a researcher in civil and geotechnical engineering at the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences. My work focuses on the safe, large-scale use of underground geological formations for hydrogen and other forms of energy storage. I combine multiphysics simulation, geomechanics, site evaluation, and risk assessment to study how salt caverns can support renewable energy systems.

title: Weizheng Bai
text:
Research Focus


Underground hydrogen storage
Storage capacity, operating performance, and engineering feasibility of salt caverns and other geological structures.


Seepage and leakage safety
Hydrogen transport through surrounding rock, well integrity, containment performance, and leakage risk assessment.


Intelligent underground energy systems
Artificial intelligence and data-driven methods for site screening, suitability evaluation, and operational decision-making.

title: Weizheng Bai
text:
Research Experience
National Key R&D Program of China: Applicability and Key Safety Technologies of Geological Hydrogen Storage Facilities December 2024 - November 2027
Developing evaluation indices for geological sealing and stability, integrating geological structure, rock properties, chamber geometry, stress fields, formation fluids, and environmental constraints to support favorable-block selection.
Research and Development of Key Technologies for Underground Hydrogen Storage January 2023 - December 2024
Studied hydrogen transport in surrounding rock, established a geological suitability evaluation system, and screened feasible geological sites for underground hydrogen storage in China.
